topic: Someone finds out their best friend is moving across the country
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crumpled envelope sat on my desk like a landmine. My fingers traced the embossed address—a city name so far away it felt like another planet. When I finally tore it open, the words swam before my eyes. Liam was moving. Across the country. A silence descended, thick and heavy, muffling the usual hum of the apartment. I didn't move for a long time, just staring at the ink bleeding into the cheap paper. A strange lightness bloomed in my chest, a feeling like floating just above the floor. I finally pushed myself up, pacing the small space. Every corner of the room seemed suddenly vast, the familiar furniture looking small and temporary. I needed to talk to him, but the words caught in my throat, lodging themselves somewhere deep behind my ribs.

[story 2]

I was halfway through making coffee when I saw the text notification. It was from Liam, brief and factual: "Big move. See you soon." My hand froze mid-air, the ceramic mug trembling slightly in my grip. I reread the message three times, searching for some hidden code, some inside joke that would make sense of the sudden seismic shift happening inside me. My breath hitched. I walked to the window, pressing my forehead against the cool glass. The cityscape blurred into streaks of concrete and light. It wasn't sadness I felt; it was a dizzying sense of possibility, like standing at the edge of an open ocean. My knuckles were white where I gripped the windowsill. What an immense, sprawling adventure was about to unfold for him.

[story 3]

It happened over lukewarm takeout and an evening movie. Liam casually mentioned he was looking at apartments in Denver. The air seemed to thin out in the room. I stopped chewing, the plastic wrapper suddenly feeling insignificant. I looked at him, really looked, trying to catch any flicker of deception, but there was only an open, bright expression. A slow, involuntary smile spread across my face, stretching my cheeks until it ached. My feet started tapping a quick, uneven rhythm against the hardwood floor. I felt a sudden, powerful urge to gather every single shared memory—the late-night drives, the whispered secrets—and bottle them up, just to hold onto the sheer scale of this change. It was breathtaking, this trajectory.
